business.headline: Sensex up 500 points: Why is stock market rising today?At 1:45 PM, the BSE Sensex was up 514.96 points, or 0.68%, at 75,698.32. The index touched an intraday high of 75,810.97 and a low of 75,230.75.

Is India sleepwalking into a retirement crisis?Many Indians dream of a peaceful retirement, but dreams alone do not pay medical bills or monthly expenses. So, are Indians financially ready for retirement, or just hoping for the best?

From long queues to quick clicks: How innovation is reshaping digital lendingApplying for a loan is no longer just about filling forms and waiting for approvals. From instant verification to quicker disbursals, innovation is reshaping digital lending, but what does this shift really mean for borrowers?

RBI may skip repo rate hike despite rupee fall, eye inflation: ReportThe RBI is expected to focus more on inflation and broader economic stability even as the rupee continues to hit record lows against the US dollar amid the ongoing global oil shock.

Soft landing, not free fall: Decoding RBI's rupee game planThe central bank is increasingly allowing the rupee to weaken gradually as oil prices remain high, with economists arguing that preventing panic matters more than defending a symbolic number.

8th Pay Commission consultation moves to Lucknow: Key dates, eligibility and detailsThe 8th Pay Commission is steadily expanding its consultation process, with Lucknow now added to the list of cities where employee representatives and government bodies can share their views. But who can meet the panel, and what are the key dates you should know?

Rupee at 100 against US dollar? Why experts say India shouldn't panicAs crude oil prices stay elevated and the rupee weakens further, a growing number of experts now argue India's real risk is not crossing 100 against the dollar, but rising inflation and slowing growth.

Parle Industries hits 5% upper circuit again as investors chase wrong Parle stockParle Industries shares hit the upper circuit for a third straight session after the Modi-Meloni Melody video went viral. The rally reflected confusion over the Parle name, even though Melody toffees are made by unlisted Parle Products.

Sensex, Nifty open higher as crude stays near $105; banks, financials lead gainsThe BSE Sensex was up 336.46 points at 75,519.82 around 9:26 am, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 86.30 points to trade at 23,741.00.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will markets rise on easing crude prices or stay volatile?Dalal Street is set for a near-flat start as crude hovers near $105 and traders watch US-Iran talks. Foreign selling, mixed technical signals and domestic buying are keeping the market range-bound.

Not Saudi, not US: India's newest major oil supplier may surprise youVenezuela became India's third-largest crude supplier in May, overtaking Saudi Arabia and the United States. The shift shows how lower prices and regional supply disruptions are reshaping India's crude sourcing.

Volatile markets are testing investors. Here's where smart money is movingFrom tensions in the Middle East to growing fragmentation in global trade and continued pressure on commodity prices, multiple headwinds have battered investor sentiment worldwide. However, instead of witnessing a sharp market crash, investors are seeing prolonged sideways movement and extended consolidation across global and Indian equities.

We are not the fastest growing economy, it's factually incorrect: Surjit BhallaIn an exclusive interview with India Today TV, prominent economist and former IMF executive director Surjit Bhalla discussed India's economic performance and the decline in foreign direct investment since 2015.

India-US partnership driven by trust and shared economic growth: Piyush GoyalSpeaking at the Annual Leadership Summit of the American Chamber of Commerce in India in New Delhi, Goyal said India has become a trusted partner for global businesses and is well-positioned to benefit from changing global economic trends.

AI saves 75% of my time: Anil Agarwal on why Indians should not fear new techWhat if one tool could save hours of your working day? According to Vedanta Chairman Anil Agarwal, technology is already doing exactly that. Sharing his own experience, he said it saves 75% of his time and urged Indians, especially youngsters, to embrace it rather than fear it.

Samsung chip workers could get bonuses worth nearly $400,000 amid AI boom: ReportUnder the proposed plan, bonuses will be closely linked to how well each business unit performs. Since Samsung's memory-chip business is currently benefiting from booming demand for AI memory products, employees in that segment stand to gain the most.
Sensex, Nifty erase early gains as RBI rate hike fears weigh on marketsThe BSE Sensex closed 135.03 points lower at 75,183.36, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 4.30 points to end at 23,789.00.

Why the rupee's fall could make owning a home more expensive for youThe rupee's sharp slide is raising concerns that the RBI may need to raise rates. That could lift home loan EMIs and make buying a house harder for borrowers.

Rupee's fall sparks concern. But is the whole of Asia under pressure too?The rupee has extended its losing streak as a stronger dollar, costly crude and foreign outflows weigh on it. The sharper slide against several Asian peers has turned the move into a wider inflation and trade concern.

Goods transport costs rise from today, daily essentials may soon get costlierTransporters have introduced a fuel-linked surcharge as diesel prices climb. The move could push up logistics costs for groceries, medicines and online deliveries.

entertainment.headline: Anusha Dandekar clarifies cryptic post after Karan-Tejasswi's on-screen proposal Anusha Dandekar said her cryptic Instagram post was unrelated to Karan Kundrra's proposal to Tejasswi Prakash on Desi Bling. The clarification came after online users linked her cryptic post to the couple.

The Mandalorian and Grogu review: Pedro Pascal returns, but Grogu remains the soulRelease Date:Fri May 22, 2026 The Mandalorian and Grogu review: Pedro Pascal and Grogu bring heart and emotional depth to a visually rich Star Wars adventure that values companionship over spectacle.

Pooja Bhatt takes a trip down memory lane with Aamir Khan, teases untold story Pooja Bhatt revisited memories from her Dil Hai Ke Manta Nahin era by posting old photos with Aamir Khan. She also teased an untold backstory linked to the impromptu shoot.

Drishyam 4: Why Mohanlal's Georgekutty story is far from over Drishyam 3 follows Georgekutty as he tries to end the suffering around Varun's death. But Geetha's final response suggests the family's nightmare may not be over.

He backstabbed me: Watching Michael reopened old wounds for Ram Gopal Varma Ram Gopal Varma shared a personal note on Michael Jackson after watching the film Michael. In his heartfelt post, the filmmaker mentioned how Jackson's death broke a long-held fantasy.

'Cockroach' Prakash Raj mixes mangoes and politics in viral CJP trend. Watch What began as a satirical response to a courtroom comment has snowballed into one of social media's loudest political trends. Prakash Raj's latest 'cockroach eating mangoes' video has only added more flavour to the viral moment.

Are Anirudh Ravichander and Kavya Maran getting married? Here's what we know Fresh rumours claiming Anirudh Ravichander and Kavya Maran are planning to get married have resurfaced online, though neither has confirmed the speculation.

Akshay Kumar's Rs 1 crore donation transforms a school in Kashmir border area Akshay Kumar's Rs 1 crore donation has funded a new education block at Government Middle School Nero Tulail in a remote border village of Kashmir.

Fact check: Why is Kangana Ranaut wearing a mangalsutra in viral video? Actor-politician Kangana Ranaut's latest appearance, wearing a mangalsutra, has sparked wedding rumours online. But is the actor really married, or is there another reason behind the viral look?

Lupita Nyong'o addresses hate over Helen of Troy casting in Nolan's The Odyssey Actor Lupita Nyong'o dismissed backlash over her casting as Helen of Troy in Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey, saying her focus is on the filmmaker's interpretation of the myth and exploring the character beyond beauty.

After China and Sri Lanka, Drishyam's Georgekutty will now take over Indonesia As Drishyam 3 opened in cinemas on May 21, its Indonesian remake is set for an August 20 release. The film stars Vino G Bastian and Marsha Timothy as rivals.

Kamala Harris, Jimmy Kimmel, fans react to Stephen Colbert's final Late Show gig Stephen Colbert hosted the final episode of The Late Show with Stephen Colbert, ending his 11-year run on CBS and drawing emotional reactions online. Celebrities including Kamala Harris, Conan O'Brien and Jimmy Kimmel paid tribute to the late-night host following the farewell broadcast.

Konkona unfollows Cockroach Janta Party? Anurag, Dia still follow Konkona Sen Sharma has unfollowed the Cockroach Janata Party account after initially following the viral satire page. However, celebrities like director Anurag Kashyap and actor Dia Mirza are still following the account.

Jake Johnson wants to be Bollywood's hero: I want the dance number, everything Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed actors Jake Johnson and Jessy Hodges discussed playing morally complicated characters in an interview. The chat then veered into a playful but earnest pitch for an India-set project and a Bollywood dance number.

Karuppu box office Day 7: Suriya film smashes Rs 200 crore globally in 1st week Suriya and Trisha Krishnan's Karuppu has grossed Rs 207 crore worldwide in seven days. The film stayed steady on its first Thursday, with the Tamil version leading the run.

Pati Patni...box office Day 7: Film fails to beat predecessor's 1st week collection Pati Patni Aur Woh Do ended its first week in theatres with a gradual decline in collections across weekdays. The drop leaves the film trailing its predecessor and facing fresh pressure from new releases this Friday.

System review: Sonakshi Sinha, Jyotika's legal drama is sensitive but lacks impactRelease Date:Fri May 22, 2026 System movie review: Prime Video's System follows young lawyer Neha and court stenographer Sarika as they navigate a key case. Their bond gives the film a sharp lens on class, patriarchy and unequal access to justice.

Ronit Roy warns fans of cyber scam using his identity, shares details Ronit Roy has warned fans about scammers allegedly using his identity to contact people, especially women, and demand money for bookings and meetings with the actor.

Mohanlal's Georgekutty, the man who survived odds but lost himself by Drishyam 3 Across the Drishyam films, Mohanlal's Georgekutty outsmarts the police while trying to protect his family. By the third film, that survival has left him broken by fear, lies and grief.

general news.headline: Twisha Sharma death: High Court accepts demand for 2nd autopsy by AIIMS Delhi teamThe development came a few days after a Bhopal court dismissed a plea seeking a second post-mortem examination at AIIMS Delhi.
Twisha Sharma's husband, on the run since May 12, ready to surrender, says lawyerTwisha Sharma's husband, on the run for 10 days after her death, to surrender, says lawyer

Cockroach Janta Party linked to AAP? Ex-civil servant quits after questioning linkAs the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) rapidly gains traction online, questions are mounting whether is linked to the Aam Aadmi Party. A former civil servant who joined the CJP quit after putting a question on its independence and getting no reply from its founder, Abhijeet Dipke, even after 24 hours.

All 50 hottest cities in the world today are in India. List hereFriday morning rankings showed all 50 of the world's hottest cities were in India, led by Balangir at 45°C. The list underlined the scale of the 2026 heatwave as the IMD kept severe alerts in place across several states.

A techie trained to kill Rajiv Gandhi in US, but then Haryana CM distracted himIn 1985, Khalistani terrorists in the US wanted to assassinate Rajiv Gandhi during his first visit to the country. Software engineer-turned-terrorist Gurpratap Singh Birk and his aides attended a terror camp in the US. But the terrorists got distracted by then Haryana CM Bhajan Lal. The LTTE assassinated Rajiv six years later.

How Trinamool's 'golden boy' dragged a senior Kolkata cop into ED's netAfter the BJP's election win, the ED has launched an aggressive crackdown on criminal syndicates that the party claims are in cahoots with the TMC. At the heart of the probe lies a startling nexus between a senior Kolkata cop and a notorious criminal believed to be close to TMC.

Not Saudi, not US: India's newest major oil supplier may surprise youVenezuela became India's third-largest crude supplier in May, overtaking Saudi Arabia and the United States. The shift shows how lower prices and regional supply disruptions are reshaping India's crude sourcing.

Mohanlal's Georgekutty, the man who survived odds but lost himself by Drishyam 3Across the Drishyam films, Mohanlal's Georgekutty outsmarts the police while trying to protect his family. By the third film, that survival has left him broken by fear, lies and grief.

Bandra slums demolition was decades overdue. But has Railways got the timing wrong?Western Railway demolished hundreds of houses and shops in Bandra's Garib Nagar after a court-backed clearance drive. A section of people backed the drive as long-overdue for safety and growing infrastructure needs. Others have criticised the timing as the displaced families will have no roof over their head during the monsoon.

Honda City facelift launched at Rs 11.99 lakh with updated styling and new featuresHonda Cars India has launched the 2026 Honda City facelift with updated styling, new features and hybrid technology, while also debuting the global ZR-V e:HEV SUV in the Indian market.

Bengal man who sold jhalmuri to PM gets bomb, death threats from Pak, BangladeshBikram Sau, the Bengal jhalmuri seller who shot to fame after Prime Minister Narendra Modi visited his stall during the election campaign, has alleged receiving bomb and death threats from Pakistani and Bangladeshi numbers. Local police have launched an investigation into the matter.

2021 post-poll violence tweet returns to haunt Bengal actorsThe complaint cited a May 2, 2021 social media exchange between actors Parambrata Chatterjee and Swastika Mukherjee, alleging their remarks encouraged violence at a time when attacks on BJP workers were being reported across Bengal following the Assembly election results.

I deleted Instagram for 30 days, what happened next is shockingI deleted Instagram for 30 days after realising reels were quietly eating away hours of my evenings. What started as a random decision ended up changing the way I spent my time. If you are a core Instagram user, this article is for you.

Cockroach fever spreads to Pakistan, spawns Cockroach Awami PartyThe cockroach is known for being difficult to eliminate. Following the massive online traction for India's Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), several similar satirical accounts have now emerged in Pakistan. One of them, the Cockroach Awami Party, is presenting itself as an alternative to mainstream Pakistani political parties.

Fintech CEO fires all HRs, says they were creating problems that never existedUS-based fintech company Bolt recently eliminated its entire HR department as part of a wider round of layoffs. Now, co-founder and CEO Ryan Breslow has defended the decision, saying the move was necessary for the company's turnaround and claiming the HR team was creating problems that did not exist.

CBSE revaluation fee jumps from Re 1 to Rs 69,420 in bizarre payment glitchCBSE's troubled reevaluation process has sparked fresh outrage after students reported bizarre fee fluctuations on the portal, with amounts allegedly changing from Re 1 to Rs 69,420 per subject. The latest glitch adds to days of complaints over crashing websites, payment failures and blurred answer sheet scans.

What is Thai Mangur, the banned catfish still reaching Indian markets?Thai Mangur fish is a banned invasive catfish that grows quickly and survives in harsh conditions. While profitable for fish farmers, it has raised environmental and health concerns across India.

Is India sleepwalking into a retirement crisis?Many Indians dream of a peaceful retirement, but dreams alone do not pay medical bills or monthly expenses. So, are Indians financially ready for retirement, or just hoping for the best?

Sack Education Minister: CJP seeks Pradhan's resignation amid NEET rowCockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has launched an online petition seeking Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the NEET 2026 paper leak row. The campaign has amplified student anger and renewed demands for accountability in the examination system.

Ensure Vinesh Phogat's participation in Asian Games trials: Delhi HC raps WFIDelhi High Court has slammed the Wrestling Federation of India for blocking Vinesh Phogat's path to Asian Games 2026. The court has told the board to ensure Vinesh's presence in the Asian Games selection trials, one that WFI had deemed her inelligible for.

Illish finally beats Chingri again as East Bengal end 22 years of Bangal painAfter 22 years of inherited heartbreak, corporate chaos, and near-misses, East Bengal's dramatic ISL title triumph has revived a sleeping giant. The moshal burns bright again; Kolkata's red-and-gold half has finally reclaimed its soul.

Found in Mumbai drain: Items for a fully furnished house, autorickshaw, police barricadeMumbai's pre-monsoon drain cleaning has pulled out household furniture, an autorickshaw and even a police barricade from eastern suburban nullahs this year. The recoveries show how Mumbaikars have turned the drains into landfills.

Their bond gives the film a sharp lens on class, patriarchy and unequal access to justice.

2 Indian climbers make it to Everest top, die while returningThe two Indian climbers died during the descent after successfully summiting Mount Everest, taking this season's death toll on the world's highest peak to five.

sports.headline: SRH playoff scenario: Can Pat Cummins' team finish in top 2? 3rd-placed SunRisers Hyderabad will play their final league stage game of IPL 2026 on Friday. CSK's heavy defeat to Gujarat Titans on Thursday has made a top-two finish highly unlikely for SRH, though a mathematical possibility still remains.

Arshdeep Singh under fire again: Latest reel mocking Priyansh Arya outrages fans Punjab Kings pacer Arshdeep Singh is facing criticism on social media again after a viral video involving young teammate Priyansh Arya went viral during the ongoing Indian Premier League season.

MS Dhoni scam? Kris Srikkanth accuses CSK of misleading fans Kris Srikkanth accused CSK of misleading supporters with repeated hints over MS Dhoni's fitness. He said clearer communication would have prepared fans for Dhoni missing the entire IPL 2026 season.

Neymar's fresh injury setback triggers concern in Brazil camp ahead of World Cup Brazil are facing fresh injury concerns after Neymar suffered another setback just weeks before the FIFA World Cup begins in North America on June 12.

Ensure Vinesh Phogat's participation in Asian Games trials: Delhi HC raps WFI Delhi High Court has slammed the Wrestling Federation of India for blocking Vinesh Phogat's path to Asian Games 2026. The court has told the board to ensure Vinesh's presence in the Asian Games selection trials, one that WFI had deemed her inelligible for.

Vaibhav is just 15: Dinesh Karthik warns against Sachin Tendulkar comparisons Former India wicketkeeper Dinesh Karthik has cautioned against burdening teenage batting prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi with comparisons to batting legend Sachin Tendulkar, stressing that the youngster's challenges will be very different in the modern era.

Not IPL, Test cricket for Australia is my priority: SRH captain Pat Cummins Ahead of SRH's final league stage game against RCB, captain Pat Cummins says that is No.1 priority remains Australian cricket, in particular the Test format. Cummins says that it is unlikely that his priorities change.

Pakistan continue selection circus: Babar in, Rizwan out in squad for Australia ODIs Pakistan have recalled Babar Azam and left out Mohammad Rizwan for the three-match home ODI series against Australia. Shaheen Afridi will continue to lead the side while pacer Naseem Shah returns.

Top 2 out of grasp, SRH hope to dent RCB's confidence in Hyderabad RCB vs SRH Preview: Defending champions RCB and SRH will play their final league-stage game in the IPL 2026. RCB are firmly placed at No.1 and SRH are unlikely to make it to the top 2 places due to their NRR deficit. So what's there to play for?

Illish finally beats Chingri again as East Bengal end 22 years of Bangal pain After 22 years of inherited heartbreak, corporate chaos, and near-misses, East Bengal's dramatic ISL title triumph has revived a sleeping giant. The moshal burns bright again; Kolkata's red-and-gold half has finally reclaimed its soul.

Season ends, enigma endures: CSK deliver final word on MS Dhoni's IPL future IPL 2026: After CSK's exit on Thursday, bowling coach Eric Simons said MS Dhoni alone will decide his IPL future despite missing the season with injuries. Suresh Raina's account of Dhoni saying 'let me see' keeps the retirement question alive.

IPL Play of the Day: Ahmedabad rings with Suii-raj as the Powerplay King rulesIPL 2026, GT vs CSK: Mohammed Siraj produced a fiery new-ball spell in Ahmedabad that flipped the match on its head on Thursday. His burst of wickets and trademark celebrations set the tone for Gujarat Titans' dominant performance against the Super Kings.

Drums and tears: Ronaldo celebrates Saudi league title, his X post goes viralCristiano Ronaldo scored twice as Al-Nassr won the Saudi Pro League title on Thursday in Riyadh. The 41-year-old teared up before celebrating his maiden triumph in Saudi with fans at Al-Awwal Park on Thursday.

Shubman Gill is IPL 2026's best captain: Ex-India star after GT crush CSKIPL 2026: Shubman Gill's captaincy credentials received massive backing after Gujarat Titans crushed CSK to seal a top-two finish push this season. Former India batter Mohammed Kaif called Gill the best captain of the season after another dominant GT display.

After roasting GT fielding, Gill leads by example with stunning CSK displayShubman Gill showed his team how things are done on the field by producing a brilliant run-out and catch during GT's win over CSK in Ahmedabad.

Ruturaj has no answer on Dhoni's future after CSK's 2026 exitRuturaj Gaikwad said he does not know whether MS Dhoni will return after CSK's IPL 2026 exit. The uncertainty has sharpened focus on Dhoni's future as Chennai enter a transition phase.

Sai Sudharsan joins Virender Sehwag in elite IPL club with special fifty vs CSKIPL 2026: Sai Sudharsan's dream IPL 2026 season continued in Ahmedabad as the Gujarat Titans opener joined Virender Sehwag in an elite IPL batting club. The left-hander became only the second Indian ever to score five consecutive IPL fifties during GT's clash against Chennai Super Kings.

CSK crash and burn in Ahmedabad, exit IPL 2026 with 89-run loss to GTChennai Super Kings suffered an 89-run loss to get eliminated from the IPL 2026 race as Gujarat Titans strengthened their position to finish in the top two. Gujarat's top three scored fifties while Rashid Khan, Mohammed Siraj and Kagiso Rabada picked up three wickets each to wreck the Chennai batting order.

Harry Maguire reacts to England World Cup snub, will Cole Palmer follow suit?Harry Maguire and Cole Palmer are among the notable players reportedly left out of England's World Cup squad by Thomas Tuchel, alongside other high-profile omissions. Meanwhile, several younger players including Kobbie Mainoo, Tino Livramento and Noni Madueke are set to be selected for the tournament.

Para archer Sheetal Devi's coach given jail term in sexual harrassment caseA Sonipat fast-track court sentenced India para archery coach Kuldeep Vedwan to five years in prison under the POCSO Act. Vedwan played a big role in Sheetal Devi's rise in para archery.

Prince Yadav's father reveals sacrifice behind India ODI call-upPrince Yadav's father recalled the sacrifices and tennis-ball tournaments behind his maiden India ODI call-up. The selection capped years of struggle and marked a major step in India's pace transition.

East Bengal clinch maiden ISL title, Mohun Bagan suffer front-row painTwenty-two years of hurt, chaos, collapses and heartbreak finally melted away for East Bengal on a wild Kolkata night. And to make it even sweeter for the red-and-gold faithful, the title arrived with Mohun Bagan falling behind them in the race that mattered most.

After Kohli's fitness remark, Manpreet invites RCB star for training challengeManpreet Singh has invited Virat Kohli and the RCB squad for a joint training session and yo-yo test. The exchange followed Kohli's admission that Indian cricketers are nowhere near hockey players in terms of fitness.

Rajat Patidar back in time for RCB's top-two push vs SRH, confirms Mo BobatIPL 2026: Mo Bobat confirmed Rajat Patidar is fit again and training before RCB's match against Sunrisers Hyderabad. His return sharpens Bengaluru's push for a top-two finish as Bobat also highlighted Bhuvneshwar Kumar's value across the dressing room.

National anti-doping act to be amended, jail term proposed for drug suppliersThe Centre has proposed amending the National Anti-Doping Act to criminalise the supply and trafficking of banned substances to athletes. The move hardens India's anti-doping stance as its poor WADA record collides with major hosting ambitions.

Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli doing everything to last till 2027 World Cup: Ricky PontingRicky Ponting believes Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma are still pushing hard towards one final ODI World Cup run in 2027. While Kohli continues dominating with the bat, Rohit's biggest challenge may now be whether his body can keep up with another two-year cycle.

We are Champions daddy: How Arteta learnt Arsenal ended 22-year EPL waitArsenal sealed the Premier League title after Manchester City drew with Bournemouth. The moment ended a 22-year wait and allowed Arsenal to shift attention towards the Champions League final.

Afghanistan women's refugee team to tour England under ECB initiativeThe (ECB) will host an Afghanistan Refugee Women's Team for a tour beginning on June 22 under a special initiative aimed at supporting displaced Afghan cricketers and promoting inclusivity in the sport.

Why drop Hosein? CSK fans left fuming as spinner dropped for Short vs GTChennai Super Kings left out Akeal Hosein and brought in Matt Short for a must-win game against Gujarat Titans. The call jarred with supporters because Hosein had been one of CSK's more effective bowlers while the playoff equation remained unforgiving.

World Cup: Germany recall Manuel Neuer, 40, as first-choice goalkeeperGermany coach Julian Nagelsmann has brought veteran goalkeeper Manuel Neuer out of international retirement and reinstated him as the country's first-choice goalkeeper for the upcoming World Cup. The decision marks a major shift in Germany's plans after Oliver Baumann had been backed as the No. 1 following Euro 2024.

French Open 2026 draw: Jannik Sinner, Novak Djokovic learn about path to titleJannik Sinner and Novak Djokovic have landed on opposite sides of the French Open 2026 draw. That keeps open the prospect of a final between the top seed chasing a Career Grand Slam and Djokovic hunting a 25th major.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
